channel.title: Luhui Dev (en)
channel.description: Essays on AI systems for structured thinking

item.title: DSPy Tutorial: Why Signatures Are Easier to Optimize Than Raw Prompts
item.description: A practical DSPy tutorial for AI builders in North America. Learn how DSPy Signatures, Modules, and Optimizers improve prompt engineering, prompt optimization, and LLM pipeline design for production AI workflows.

item.title: PaperBanana Explained: How Multi-Agent AI Generates Academic Method Diagrams
item.description: A technical breakdown of PaperBanana's Retriever, Planner, Stylist, Visualizer, and Critic agents, showing how multi-agent AI generates academic method diagrams for research writing and scientific figure workflows.

item.title: How Use Multi-Agent Collaboration for Academic Figure Generation?
item.description: A focused breakdown of how PaperBanana coordinates Retriever, Planner, Stylist, Visualizer, and Critic agents to generate academic method diagrams.

item.title: AlphaGeometry DSL Guide: Google Geometry DSL, defs.txt Actions, and Predicates
item.description: A technical guide to AlphaGeometry DSL covering problem format, defs.txt action definitions, geometric predicates, rules.txt reasoning, and construction workflow for geometry solvers, dataset generation, and AlphaGeometry reproduction.

item.title: AlphaGeometry2 Deep Dive: How Does Google AI Solve IMO Geometry Problems?
item.description: How AlphaGeometry2 combines geometry reasoning, auxiliary-line search, and symbolic deduction to solve IMO geometry problems, with engineering takeaways for math AI builders.

item.title: Google DeepMind Aletheia: A Deep Dive into a Fully Autonomous Math Research Agent
item.description: From AlphaGo to AlphaGeometry, AlphaProof, and Aletheia, this essay unpacks the engineering value of a verifiable autonomous math research loop.

item.title: HKU CodePlot-CoT Deep Dive: Visual Reasoning or Geometric Reasoning?
item.description: From executable plotting to geometric proof, a structural analysis of CodePlot-CoT's strengths and boundaries.

item.title: AI and Math: Technical Paths, Frontiers, and What Comes Next (2026)
item.description: A 2026 view of AI for math, from benchmark credibility and capability tiers to architecture-heavy solving systems and real product opportunities.
